requirement_text: When a registered user submits the login form with a valid email and correct password, the system shall authenticate the user and redirect them to their personal dashboard. A session token shall be created and stored securely.

When a user submits the login form with a valid email but an incorrect password, the system shall display a generic error message without revealing whether the email exists. The failed attempt shall be logged, and the password field shall be cleared.

An administrator user shall be able to permanently delete any project from the system. Before deletion, the system shall display a confirmation dialog. Upon confirmation, the project and all its tasks shall be removed, and all project members shall receive a notification.

A user creating a new task shall be able to leave the assignee field blank. The task shall be saved successfully in an unassigned state and shall appear in the team's unassigned task view. The assignee may be set at any time after creation.

When a user attempts to register with an email address that is already associated with an existing account, the system shall display a clear error message indicating the email is already in use. The existing account shall not be modified.

A user who has forgotten their password shall be able to request a password reset link via email. The link shall expire after 60 minutes and may only be used once. Upon successful reset, the old password shall be invalidated immediately.

A user with the 'member' role shall not be able to access the admin dashboard. Any attempt to navigate to an admin URL shall result in an access-denied response, a redirect to the user's own dashboard, and the attempt shall be recorded in the audit log.

The task search feature shall correctly handle queries that contain special characters including quotes, angle brackets, ampersands, and SQL metacharacters. Results shall be returned safely and the search term shall be displayed back to the user without modification to the original input intent.
The system should send notifications to users.
Users can upgrade their account to premium to unlock additional features.

When a user has enabled two-factor authentication on their account, after entering a valid email and password, the system shall prompt the user for a time-based one-time password (TOTP). Access shall be granted only after a valid TOTP code is submitted. An invalid or expired TOTP code shall display an error without granting access.

When a user successfully authenticates, the system shall generate a new session identifier and invalidate any session that existed prior to authentication. The pre-authentication session token shall not be usable after login completes.

When a user chooses to sign in via a supported third-party OAuth provider, the system shall redirect the user to the provider's authorization page. Upon the provider returning a successful authorization, the system shall create a new account or log the user into an existing linked account and redirect them to their dashboard.

A user with the 'viewer' role in a project shall be able to view tasks, comments, and project details but shall not be able to create tasks, edit tasks, delete tasks, or add comments. Any attempt to perform a write action shall be rejected with an access-denied error.

A user with the 'editor' role in a project shall be able to create tasks, edit any task in the project, and add comments. An editor shall not be able to delete the project itself or modify project-level settings.

An organization-level admin shall be able to create, configure, and delete any project within the organization and manage any user's organization membership. A project-level admin shall only be able to manage the specific project they administer and shall not be able to access or modify other projects within the organization.

When a user is removed from a project, the system shall immediately revoke their access to that project's tasks and content. If the user has an active session at the time of removal, they shall receive an access-denied response on their next request to a project resource and shall be redirected. No grace period shall be granted.

A user shall be able to select multiple tasks using checkboxes and perform bulk operations: reassign to a different user, change status, and delete. Bulk delete shall display a confirmation dialog showing the count of tasks to be deleted. All selected tasks shall be updated atomically.

A task shall support an optional due date field. When the current date is past the task's due date and the task status is not 'done', the system shall display an overdue indicator on the task. Completed tasks shall not show an overdue indicator regardless of due date.

A user shall be able to add one or more subtasks to an existing task. Each subtask shall have a title and a completion status. The parent task shall display a progress indicator showing the number of completed subtasks out of total subtasks. Completing all subtasks shall not automatically complete the parent task.

The task search feature shall return results in pages of 25 items per page. Each search response shall include the total number of matching results and the current page number. The user shall be able to navigate to any valid page number.

The task search feature shall support simultaneous filtering by task status (one or more values), assignee (one or more), and due date range (start date and end date). When multiple filters are active, only tasks matching ALL active filters shall be returned. An empty result set shall not be returned when tasks exist and no filters are active.

A user shall be able to enable a daily notification digest mode. In digest mode, individual event notifications shall be batched and delivered in a single email at a user-configured time each day. The digest shall include all events since the previous digest delivery. If no events occurred, no digest email shall be sent.

A user shall be able to independently enable or disable notifications for each available delivery channel (email, in-app, push notification). Disabling one channel shall not affect notification delivery to other enabled channels. Changes to channel preferences shall take effect immediately for new notifications.

When a premium user downgrades to the free tier, premium-only features shall become inaccessible immediately upon downgrade confirmation. If the user's current usage exceeds free tier limits (e.g. more than 3 active projects), the content exceeding the limit shall become read-only. The user shall see a clear indication of which items are affected and shall be able to delete them to restore write access.

The system shall automatically generate a PDF invoice for each completed billing period. Each invoice shall contain the user's name, billing address, plan name, billing period dates, amount charged, and a unique invoice number. Invoices shall be available in the user's billing history and downloadable as PDF.

The REST API shall enforce rate limiting of 100 requests per minute per authenticated user. When the rate limit is exceeded, the API shall return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header indicating the number of seconds until the rate limit resets. Requests from unauthenticated users shall be limited by IP address.

The REST API shall support versioning via a URL path prefix (e.g. /v1/, /v2/). Requests to a deprecated but still active API version shall receive a valid response plus a Deprecation response header and an X-API-Sunset-Date header. Requests to an unsupported or nonexistent version shall receive HTTP 404.

All API error responses shall use a consistent JSON structure containing exactly three fields: 'error_code' (a machine-readable string identifier), 'message' (a human-readable description), and 'request_id' (a unique identifier for the request). HTTP status codes shall correspond to the error type (4xx for client errors, 5xx for server errors).

All REST API list endpoints shall support cursor-based pagination. Each response shall include a 'next_cursor' field and a 'prev_cursor' field in the response envelope. When 'next_cursor' is null or absent, the response represents the last page of results. Clients shall use the cursor value to retrieve the next or previous page.

The task filter endpoint (GET /tasks?filter=...) shall correctly handle filter parameter values containing SQL metacharacters including single quotes, double quotes, semicolons, and SQL comment sequences (-- and /**/). The endpoint shall return safe results without generating a 500 error, leaking database error messages, or executing injected SQL.

All REST API endpoints except /health and /auth/token shall require authentication via a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Requests without a token shall receive HTTP 401 Unauthorized. Requests with an expired or invalid token shall also receive HTTP 401. The /health and /auth/token endpoints shall be accessible without authentication.

When an API request body fails JSON schema validation (missing required fields, incorrect field types, or values outside allowed ranges), the API shall return HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity. The response body shall list each validation error as a structured array, including the field name, the invalid value, and a description of the constraint violated.

A user shall be able to export all tasks in a project as a CSV file by clicking an export button in the project view. The exported file shall include a header row and one data row per task with columns for task ID, title, status, assignee, due date, and created date. The export shall handle up to 10,000 tasks and complete within 30 seconds.

A user shall be able to export a project's task data as a JSON file. The exported JSON shall conform to the documented API response schema for the tasks list endpoint. A project with no tasks shall export an empty JSON array. The exported filename shall follow the pattern '{project-name}-tasks-{date}.json'.

For export requests on datasets larger than 50,000 rows, the system shall process the export asynchronously. The user shall receive an immediate acknowledgment response with a job ID. The system shall notify the user via email and in-app notification when the export is ready. The download link shall be valid for 48 hours from generation. Concurrent exports for the same user shall be limited to one active export job at a time.

When a user exports data from a project whose name contains non-ASCII characters (e.g. Chinese characters, Arabic script, or emoji), the downloaded file shall have a correctly encoded filename that can be opened by modern browsers and operating systems without modification. The system shall use RFC 5987 encoding for the Content-Disposition header.

When a new user successfully completes account registration, the system shall send a welcome email to the registered email address within 5 minutes. The welcome email shall include the user's display name, a link to the getting started guide, and a link to profile settings. If the welcome email fails to send, the registration shall still succeed and the failure shall be logged.

After first login, a new user shall be presented with a multi-step onboarding wizard covering: creating a first project, inviting a team member, and creating a first task. Progress through the wizard shall be saved after each completed step. If the user leaves and returns within 30 days, the wizard shall resume from the last completed step. Completing all steps shall display a confirmation screen.

A user shall be able to skip individual steps of the onboarding wizard or skip the entire wizard at any point during onboarding. Skipping a step shall mark it as skipped without completing it. Skipping the entire wizard shall dismiss it permanently for that user account. After skipping, the user shall have immediate full access to all system features. The skipped wizard shall not reappear on subsequent logins.
